pattern: Adding one only ripples a carry leftward through trailing nines; the first non-nine digit absorbs it, and an all-nines number grows by exactly one leading digit.
hints: Start at the least significant digit. If it is less than nine you simply bump it and you are done.
A trailing nine becomes zero and pushes a carry to the next digit on its left.

Walk right to left: while the current digit is nine, set it to zero and continue; the first digit below nine gets incremented. If you fall off the left edge, every digit was nine, so prepend a leading one.

edge_inputs.description: single nine grows a new digit
carry stops at the first non-nine
interior nines absorb the carry then stop

stress.description: random valid digit arrays with no leading zero, up to the length cap
stress.note: no leading zero except the single-digit case, matching the constraint
